subject: Product Management
title: The HP Way
description: Learn The HP Way in one minute.

hook.text: Bill Hewlett and Dave Packard built HP around a radical idea: engineers should talk to customers directly.

buildup.text: HP's 'management by walking around' meant PMs didn't just sit in offices — they visited labs and customers.

discovery.text: This hands-on culture meant HP product managers understood both the technology and the market deeply.

twist.text: Most companies kept engineers and customers apart. HP's approach was considered reckless — until it worked.

climax.text: The HP Way became a management philosophy that influenced Intel, Apple, and the entire Valley.

punchline.text: Great products start where engineers meet customers.

quiz.question: What was distinctive about HP's approach to product management?
options: Engineers and PMs talked to customers directly — management by walking around
All decisions were made by executives only
